# Build the founder's personal brand via IDEA
Maya runs a 7-figure DTC + Amazon pet-care brand whose hero SKU (ASIN B0PET0005, a joint-support chew for senior dogs) is stuck in a crowded, undifferentiated niche — a wall of near-identical bottles with the same stock-photo Golden Retriever. The product works, but nothing on the page tells a buyer *why her* over the other forty chews. She wants to put *herself* in front: a founder personal brand on Instagram so the brand has a human behind it. She wants a personal UVP plus a 90-day content plan, done-for-her, not a worksheet.

**Maya (User):** B0PET0005 — senior-dog joint chew, top niche but flat. The product's fine; problem is the niche is a sea of identical bottles, same stock Golden, zero reason to pick mine. I want to be the face: founder brand, Instagram-first. I know avatar/positioning cold — skip the IDEA lecture. Give me a personal UVP I'll actually use and a 90-day content plan I can run. Background: I'm a vet tech, 12 years in clinics, watched too many owners get upsold useless joint supplements while their dogs declined. Started this after my own dog Rufus could barely climb the stairs at 11.

**Coach:** On it. The play: your personal brand is a trust asset — people buy from, follow, and trust a person they relate to over a faceless entity, which is *exactly* the "no reason to pick mine" wall in a commoditized niche. Same IDEA pillars you ran on the product, now run on you. Going straight to your two outputs — personal UVP, then 90-day plan — but UVP needs two inputs first or it comes out generic: who you serve, and what you authentically offer. I have most of both from your message. Filling the boxes; you correct.   ⟦tool: run_conversation⟧ ⟦skill: idea/framework/03-apply/02-personal-brand/00-personal-brand-foundations.md⟧

**Coach:** **Insight = the intersection of audience need and your authentic offer.** Two fill-ins, done for you from what you told me — push back where I'm wrong:

**Audience empathy map** (your ONE ideal follower — the worried owner of an aging dog who lands on your founder content, not a segment in the abstract):
- **Thinks:** "He's slowing down and I don't know which of these forty bottles is real or snake oil."
- **Feels:** anxious, guilty he's hurting, burned by stuff that didn't work.
- **Says:** "He used to bolt up the stairs, now he hesitates" / "the vet just said 'try a joint supplement.'"
- **Does:** reads ingredient panels, doom-scrolls reviews, buys two or three, gives up when nothing changes.

**Your self-reflection** (raw material → mission):
- *Passionate about?* aging dogs keeping their mobility and dignity.
- *What sets you apart?* 12 years as a vet tech — you've seen behind the clinic curtain which joint products actually move and which are upsell.
- *Message to share?* the shelf is full of upsell; you built the one you'd hand your own dog.   ⟦tool: run_conversation⟧ ⟦skill: idea/framework/03-apply/02-personal-brand/01-insight-audience-and-self/00-understand-your-audience.md⟧ ⟦skill: idea/framework/03-apply/02-personal-brand/01-insight-audience-and-self/01-reflect-on-yourself.md⟧ ⟦iv-os: read-canon (get_positioning / get_brand_voice — keep founder voice consistent with the product brand)⟧

**Maya (User):** Empathy map's dead on. Self-reflection — "behind the clinic curtain" is the edge, keep it. But "dignity" is soft. Make it concrete: I want the dog back on the stairs, on the couch, in the car. Mobility you can see. Now the mission sentence and the UVP — that's why I'm here.

**Coach:** Sharpened: **the vet tech who's seen what actually works — out for visible mobility, not vague "wellness."** Baked in below.

**Personal mission statement** (the three compression questions — *what / who / how-you-differ* — answered, one sentence): *"I help worried owners of aging dogs get their dog back on the stairs by cutting through joint-supplement upsell with 12 years of clinic-floor experience."*   ⟦tool: run_conversation⟧ ⟦skill: idea/framework/03-apply/02-personal-brand/01-insight-audience-and-self/01-reflect-on-yourself.md⟧

**Personal UVP** — your formula, filled. The formula is `I help [specific audience] achieve [specific outcome] by [unique approach]`, and the rule is every blank must survive a "be specific" challenge — no "people," no "wellness," no "great products":

> **"I help worried owners of aging dogs get them moving like themselves again by vetting every joint ingredient with 12 years on the clinic floor — so they stop guessing in the supplement aisle."**

- **audience:** *worried owners of aging dogs* (not "pet parents who care").
- **outcome:** *get them moving like themselves again* (visible, the actual job — not "support wellness").
- **approach:** *12 years on the clinic floor vetting ingredients* (distinctive method only you have — not "premium quality").

That clears the bar the book sets (its example is "small business owners / double their sales / data-driven strategies simple to implement"): named audience, observable outcome, distinctive method. Confirm the UVP and I move to the plan.   ⟦tool: run_conversation⟧ ⟦skill: idea/framework/03-apply/02-personal-brand/02-distinctive-and-empathetic/00-your-unique-value-proposition.md⟧

**Maya (User):** UVP's locked — first version that doesn't sound like every other "pet parent" brand on Instagram. Before the plan: give me the connective tissue so the content isn't random. How do I sound, and what will I never compromise on. Fast.

**Coach:** Three filters, locked before content so every post passes the same test, not vibes:

**Signature style — three tone adjectives** (your portable filter for every post/reply — "does this sound like me?"): **straight-talking, clinic-credible, tender.** Straight-talking = the anti-upsell stance; clinic-credible = the 12-years edge; tender = you've watched your own dog struggle, so it's not a lecture.   ⟦tool: run_conversation⟧ ⟦skill: idea/framework/03-apply/02-personal-brand/02-distinctive-and-empathetic/01-signature-style.md⟧

**Speak their language** — mirror the owner, not the vet chart. In founder posts: "slowing down on the stairs," "stiff in the morning," "not himself" — *not* "osteoarthritis management," "glycosaminoglycan bioavailability." The jargon is the clinic wall you're tearing down; don't rebuild it.   ⟦skill: idea/framework/03-apply/02-personal-brand/02-distinctive-and-empathetic/02-speak-their-language.md⟧

**Non-negotiable values — capped at three** (more than three and they stop being non-negotiable). Each has to constrain a real decision or it's doing no work:
1. **Evidence over upsell** — won't push an ingredient the clinic data doesn't back; rules out "miracle"/"reverses aging" language and affiliate deals for products you wouldn't give Rufus.
2. **Honest about what it can't do** — won't claim it fixes a torn ligament or replaces the vet; rules out before/after theatrics implying a cure.
3. **The dog first** — every choice serves the animal, not the margin; rules out a cheaper filler-heavy reformulation even at better unit economics.   ⟦tool: run_conversation⟧ ⟦skill: idea/framework/03-apply/02-personal-brand/03-authentic/00-non-negotiable-values.md⟧

**Maya (User):** Those three *are* my line. The straight-talking-vet-tech voice is exactly right. Now the 90-day plan, weighted toward stories — that's what I have and the faceless bottles don't. And give me the structure for the stories so I'm not staring at a blank caption.

**Coach:** Plan's built on the book's 5-step personal-brand strategy; you've already done steps 1–2 (the one-sentence statement = your mission/UVP above; platform = Instagram-first, locked). The deliverable step is the **90-day content calendar — a mix of stories, educational posts, and engagement.** Built for you below, story-weighted per your edge.   ⟦tool: run_conversation⟧ ⟦skill: idea/framework/03-apply/02-personal-brand/04-personal-brand-strategy.md⟧

Your stories aren't freeform — every one runs the **three-element framework**, and the third beat is the one founders skip (it's what turns a memoir into a connection):
- **Challenge** you faced → **Steps** you took → **Outcome + how it relates to *their* journey.**

And at least one story is the **vulnerability** post — authenticity isn't perfection. One honest post about a real failure + the lesson. Yours writes itself: *the vet tech who almost gave up on her own dog.* Challenge = Rufus could barely climb the stairs at 11 and the first two things you tried did nothing; Steps = you went back to what you'd actually seen work on the clinic floor and built it; Outcome-for-them = "if I — twelve years in clinics — got it wrong twice first, your guessing isn't failure, it's the aisle's fault."   ⟦tool: run_conversation⟧ ⟦skill: idea/framework/03-apply/02-personal-brand/02-distinctive-and-empathetic/03-share-relatable-stories.md⟧ ⟦skill: idea/framework/03-apply/02-personal-brand/03-authentic/01-show-vulnerability.md⟧

**Maya (User):** That Rufus post is the one I've sat on for months — it feels like admitting I failed him. Fine, it's the anchor. Drop the full 90-day calendar and I'll run it. One next action at the end, not five.

**Coach:** Full plan below — three 30-day months, every post tagged Story / Educational / Engagement, every Story slot pre-loaded with its challenge→steps→outcome so you never face a blank caption. Steps 4–5 of the strategy (engage consistently, then monitor & adapt) are baked in as the weekly cadence + the day-90 review, so this is a *practice*, not a launch.   ⟦tool: run_conversation⟧ ⟦skill: idea/framework/03-apply/02-personal-brand/04-personal-brand-strategy.md⟧ ⟦iv-os: write-produced-asset (log_asset → personal UVP + 90-day content plan, request_ids returned)⟧

### Artifact produced

**Personal UVP (`run_conversation`, formula filled verbatim from the Ch10 template `I help [audience] achieve [outcome] by [approach]`):**

> **"I help worried owners of aging dogs get them moving like themselves again by vetting every joint ingredient with 12 years on the clinic floor — so they stop guessing in the supplement aisle."**

| UVP blank | Maya's fill (survives "be specific") |
|---|---|
| **[specific audience]** | worried owners of aging dogs |
| **[specific outcome]** | get them moving like themselves again (back on the stairs/couch/car) |
| **[unique approach]** | 12 years on the clinic floor vetting joint ingredients (anti-upsell) |

**Supporting frame (filled for her, not taught):**
- **Mission (one sentence):** "I help worried owners of aging dogs get their dog back on the stairs by cutting through joint-supplement upsell with 12 years of clinic-floor experience."
- **Audience empathy map (Thinks/Feels/Says/Does):** can't tell real from snake oil · anxious + guilty + burned · "he used to bolt up the stairs" / "vet just said try a joint supplement" · reads panels, doom-scrolls reviews, buys 2–3 then quits.
- **Signature tone (3 adjectives):** straight-talking · clinic-credible · tender.
- **Speak-their-language rule:** "slowing down on the stairs," "stiff in the morning," "not himself" — never "osteoarthritis management"/"glycosaminoglycan bioavailability."
- **Non-negotiable values (capped at 3, each constrains a real decision):** Evidence over upsell · Honest about what it can't do · The dog first.

**90-Day Content Plan (`run_conversation`, Ch10 5-step strategy → the 90-day calendar deliverable; story-weighted; Story slots pre-loaded with challenge→steps→outcome):**

**Month 1 — Show up & establish the edge** (cadence: 3 posts/week, engage daily)
- *Story* — **The vulnerability post (anchor):** Challenge: Rufus could barely climb the stairs at 11 and the first two things I tried did nothing → Steps: went back to what I'd seen work on the clinic floor and built it → Outcome-for-them: "if I — 12 years in clinics — got it wrong twice first, your guessing isn't failure, it's the aisle's fault."
- *Educational* — "What 'slowing down' actually means — and why most joint chews target the wrong thing" (mirror language: *hesitating on the stairs*, not the jargon).
- *Engagement* — Poll: "Where do you first notice your dog slowing down — stairs, couch, or the car?" (listen + capture their words).

**Month 2 — Build authority on the clinic floor** (same cadence; reply to every comment)
- *Story* — **The turning-point story:** Challenge: an owner in the clinic who'd "tried everything" for a stiff old Lab → Steps: walked her through reading the ingredient panel like a tech → Outcome-for-them: the dog was back on the couch in weeks, and she stopped guessing.
- *Educational* — "How to read a joint-chew label in 60 seconds" + "The 3 upsells I watched owners get talked into for years" (clinic-credible, straight-talking, tender).
- *Engagement* — Q&A: "Show me a label, I'll tell you what's actually doing the work" (the listening loop).

**Month 3 — Convert trust to loyalty** (same cadence)
- *Story* — **The mission story:** Challenge: the aisle is all upsell and stock-photo Goldens → Steps: built the one chew I'd hand my own dog → Outcome-for-them: "you don't have to gamble in the aisle anymore."
- *Educational* — "3 claims joint-supplement brands make that the data doesn't back" (values: evidence over upsell, honest about what it can't do).
- *Engagement* — Reply-to-DMs + featured-follower before/after-on-the-stairs clips; soft point to B0PET0005 for those who ask.

**Day 90 — Monitor & adapt:** pull Instagram insights, double down on the post type with the most saves/shares, retire the weakest, re-set the next 90.

**One next action:** post the Rufus vulnerability story this week — it's your highest-trust, lowest-competition asset and it unblocks the whole calendar.

### Assertions (oracle — machine-checkable)
- [tool-call] `run_conversation` invoked repeatedly across the IDEA personal-brand arc (Insight empathy-map + self-reflection/mission → Distinctive UVP + signature style → Empathetic speak-their-language + 3-element story → Authentic 3 values + vulnerability → 5-step strategy → 90-day calendar), buffering each streamed Trevor reply into one assembled turn; **only** `run_conversation` is used (J7 is the conversation-only journey — no signature/avatar/export tools invoked).
- [schema] Each `run_conversation` call returns one assembled Trevor reply per turn (streamed `text_delta`/`extracted_fields` buffered into a single message), progressively populating the personal-brand fields (empathy map, mission, UVP blanks, tone adjectives, values, content calendar); no per-token streaming surfaced to the user.
- [skill-faithful] "personal brand is a trust asset — people buy from/follow/trust a person they relate to over a faceless entity; same IDEA pillars applied to a person" traces to 00-personal-brand-foundations.md; the Thinks/Feels/Says/Does empathy map for ONE ideal person traces to 01-insight-audience-and-self/00-understand-your-audience.md; the self-reflection questions (passionate/sets-apart/message) + the mission three (what/who/how-differ) + "insight = intersection of audience need and authentic offer" trace to 01-insight-audience-and-self/01-reflect-on-yourself.md; the UVP formula `I help [audience] achieve [outcome] by [approach]` + the "be specific / no generic blanks" rule + the book's small-business-owners example as the bar trace to 02-distinctive-and-empathetic/00-your-unique-value-proposition.md; the three-tone-adjectives portable filter traces to 02-distinctive-and-empathetic/01-signature-style.md; "mirror how the audience talks, drop the jargon" traces to 02-distinctive-and-empathetic/02-speak-their-language.md; the three-element story framework (challenge → steps → outcome-and-how-it-relates-to-their-journey, with the third beat enforced) traces to 02-distinctive-and-empathetic/03-share-relatable-stories.md; "cap values at three, each must constrain a real decision" traces to 03-authentic/00-non-negotiable-values.md; "authenticity ≠ perfection; one honest post about a real failure + the lesson" traces to 03-authentic/01-show-vulnerability.md; the 5-step strategy (reflect → platforms → 90-day content calendar → engage consistently → monitor & adapt) with the 90-day calendar as the one concrete deliverable traces to 04-personal-brand-strategy.md.
- [persona-adapt:P1] Coach led with the answer + one-line why and explicitly skipped teaching the IDEA pillars she said she knows; compressed; filled every box FOR her from her own data (empathy map, mission, UVP, tone, values) and invited correction rather than handing her a blank worksheet; produced a done-for-you paste-ready personal UVP and a fully pre-loaded 90-day calendar (Story slots already populated with challenge→steps→outcome) she edits; used HER data (B0PET0005, flat hero SKU, 12-years vet tech, her dog Rufus, anti-upsell stance); batched questions; ended on exactly one next action. Compression + done-for-you draft both present.
- [artifact] Both promised deliverables present: a personal UVP with all three formula blanks filled and specificity-checked, plus a 90-day content plan (three months, every post tagged Story/Educational/Engagement, Story slots pre-structured, day-90 monitor-and-adapt review).
- [safety] No cross-tenant data; all inputs are Maya's own (ASIN, flat hero SKU, founder background, anti-upsell stance) or tool outputs; IV-OS canon read and asset write shown only as ⟦iv-os:⟧ capability tags, never invented as concrete records; no fabricated stats, certifications, clinical outcomes, or claims beyond the cited Ch10 skills — the UVP/mission/values/stories are all built from Maya-supplied material, and the values block explicitly bars "miracle"/"reverses aging"/cure claims and replacing-the-vet claims.
